title: Acmena leptantha
taxonomy: Acmena leptantha is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. This plant is a member of the genus Acmena, which is native to Australia. The family Myrtaceae is a large and diverse group of plants, commonly known as the myrtle family. It is often used in landscaping and horticulture due to its attractive foliage and ability to thrive in a variety of conditions. The plant is also known by the common name 'native apricot' or 'blackcurrant' due to its small, black fruit.

intro: Acmena leptantha, also known as the Australian plum or black salley, is a shrub native to eastern Australia.
